rationale.policy: Each limit is a hard raw-byte/entry ceiling enforced by viteBudgetPlugin during the production build (Rollup generateBundle) and the Workbox precache manifest transform. One byte or one entry over any limit fails the build. Baselines are the current measured actuals plus a small (~4-5%) headroom so routine edits do not flap CI while a material regression is caught.
rationale.initialEntryBytes: Raw bytes of the synchronously-loaded entry chunk (index-*.js). The web-architecture plan's original target was 423707, baselined against a smaller plan-time entry; tasks 1-9 grew the eager entry (pure state cores + consolidated grammar scanner are imported at startup) to ~440910 raw (measured 2026-07-16 in generateBundle), ABOVE that 423707 target. Every other budget here matches the plan number exactly (each is actual + ~4-5% headroom); 423707 alone is stale because the real entry outgrew it, so enforcing it literally would make the build permanently red rather than catch regressions. Rebaselined to the current actual + ~4.8% headroom, consistent with the headroom methodology of the other five budgets. See task-10-report.md for the full discrepancy write-up; the 462016 rebaseline was ratified by the user on 2026-07-16 as the permanent limit (no entry-shrink follow-up owed). Re-measured for the PDF viewer feature (pkm-srek, 2026-07-16): actual is 445507, ~1.04% above the ~440910 reference above but still comfortably under this 462016 limit; the growth traces to PdfEmbed.tsx's fallback-link wrapper logic and the new PdfFallbackLink.tsx component, both of which must render eagerly (before the lazy viewer chunk loads) — PdfViewer.tsx itself (which pulls in react-pdf/pdfjs-dist) is only reached via the dynamic `import("./PdfViewer")` in PdfEmbed.tsx, so no PDF-library code leaked into the eager entry. Limit intentionally left unchanged. Re-measured for the LaTeX support feature (pkm-lr96, 2026-07-17): actual is 451914, +6407 (~1.4%) above the 445507 pkm-srek reference, still comfortably under this 462016 limit; the growth was ASSERTED (not measured) to trace to the eagerly-rendered $$...$$ tokenizer (grammar/tokenize.ts) and the MathSpan.tsx/InlineSegments.tsx dispatch wrapper. Limit intentionally left unchanged. Follow-up source-map-explorer measurement (pkm-zotb, 2026-07-19) corrects that assertion: built sourcemapped index-*.js for the commit immediately before the pkm-lr96 merge (24690e6) and the merge commit itself (3b67c19), then diffed source-map-explorer's per-file breakdown of the two. The true LaTeX-only contribution to the eager entry is just +1389 bytes (MathSpan.tsx +758 as a new file, grammar/tokenize.ts +463 for the $$ delimiter scan, InlineSegments.tsx +58 for the dispatch wiring, +110 unmapped/EOL noise) -- an order of magnitude below the +6407 previously attributed to it. The other ~5KB of the +6407 had already landed on main BEFORE the LaTeX merge: 24690e6 (pkm-aze9, "expand uploaded images in a modal", 2026-07-17) measures 451961 raw on its own, already +6454 over the 445507 pkm-srek reference, with LaTeX not yet merged. The image-expansion modal's eagerly-rendered code was misattributed to LaTeX only because the two measurements happened to straddle it. KaTeX itself (~260KB per copy) is confirmed to never enter the eager entry at all (only reached via the dynamic `import("katex")` in MathSpan.tsx's loadKatex(); katexOwnedBytes below captures the library bytes separately with no corresponding jump here). Re-measured again for KaTeX bundle hygiene (pkm-zotb, 2026-07-19, current main): actual is 457509, +5595 above the 451914 pkm-lr96 reference from further unrelated upstream drift (additional beans merged into main between 2026-07-17 and 2026-07-19), not from this task's katex dedupe/@types removal. Still comfortably under this 462016 limit (~1% headroom). Limit intentionally left unchanged.
rationale.largestAssetBytes: Largest single output file. Now set by the pdf.js worker asset (app-assets/pdf.worker.min-*.mjs), not the sqlite3 wasm binary that used to hold this record (~864752): the worker is an emitted .mjs asset outside Rollup's chunk graph and measures ~1046214 raw (measured 2026-07-16, pkm-srek PDF viewer). Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (1093294) for a minor pdf.js version bump without silently precaching a much larger binary. Re-measured for KaTeX bundle hygiene (pkm-zotb, 2026-07-19): actual is unchanged at 1046214 (the pdf.js worker is untouched by the katex dedupe). Limit intentionally left unchanged.
rationale.totalOutputBytes: Sum of every emitted output file. Grew to 6782567 raw (measured 2026-07-16) from the pre-PDF-viewer 5520272 limit because the PDF viewer family (react-pdf + pdfjs-dist lazy chunk, plus the pdf.js worker asset) joined the build. Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (7087783). Guards against overall bundle bloat regardless of chunk split. Grew again to 8153042 raw (measured 2026-07-17, pkm-lr96 LaTeX support) from the pre-KaTeX 7087783 limit: two KaTeX JS chunks (~260KB each -- see katexOwnedBytes for why there are two), a KaTeX CSS chunk (~30KB), and ~60 individual KaTeX font files (woff/woff2/ttf for every face, not just the ~11 core faces Workbox precaches -- Vite emits every font url() the CSS references) all joined the build. Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (8519929). Shrank to 7899485 raw (measured 2026-07-19, pkm-zotb KaTeX bundle hygiene) from the pre-dedupe 8153042 reference: the pnpm override (`overrides: { katex: ^0.17.0 }` in pnpm-workspace.yaml) collapsed mermaid's transitive katex@0.16.47 onto the app's own katex@0.17.0, dropping the second ~260KB katex-*.js chunk and its duplicate CSS/font set. Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (8254962).
rationale.precacheBytes: Sum of the Workbox precache manifest entry sizes. This is the offline app-shell weight a cold install downloads. Grew to 6790858 raw (measured 2026-07-16) from the pre-PDF-viewer 5494604 limit once the pdf.js worker asset and the PDF viewer chunk joined the precache manifest (the viewer must work offline). Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (7096447). Must stay bounded so install stays fast and quota-safe. Grew again to 7250342 raw (measured 2026-07-17, pkm-lr96 LaTeX support) from the pre-KaTeX 7096447 limit: the two KaTeX JS chunks, the KaTeX CSS chunk, and 10 of the 11 core-face woff2 fonts added by vite.config.ts's new globPattern (KaTeX_Size3-Regular.woff2 is only 3.6KB, under Vite's 4096-byte assetsInlineLimit, so it is inlined as a base64 data URI inside the KaTeX CSS chunk instead of emitted as a standalone file -- still fully precached, just not as its own manifest entry) all joined the manifest. Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (7576607). Must stay bounded so install stays fast and quota-safe. Shrank to 6996785 raw (measured 2026-07-19, pkm-zotb KaTeX bundle hygiene) from the pre-dedupe 7250342 reference: only one katex-*.js chunk (and its CSS/fonts) now enters the Workbox precache manifest. Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (7311640).
rationale.precacheEntries: Number of precache manifest entries. Measured 76 (2026-07-16) with the PDF viewer family in the manifest; rebaselined to actual + 3 (79), tighter than the prior 82 limit, since the real entry count did not grow as much as the PDF worker+chunk addition alone would suggest (offset by unrelated upstream chunk consolidation). Guards against an explosion of precached files (e.g. a new eagerly-split dependency family). Measured 88 (2026-07-17, pkm-lr96 LaTeX support) with the KaTeX family in the manifest: +13 KaTeX-related entries (2 JS chunks + 1 CSS chunk + 10 standalone core-face woff2 fonts; the 11th core face, Size3, is inlined into the CSS chunk per precacheBytes above) offset by -4 elsewhere from unrelated upstream chunk-splitting churn, net +9 over the 79 baseline. Rebaselined to actual + 3 (91). Shrank to 87 (measured 2026-07-19, pkm-zotb KaTeX bundle hygiene) from the pre-dedupe 88 reference: the duplicate katex-*.js chunk and its distinct CSS/font entries dropped out of the manifest, net -4 after unrelated upstream chunk-splitting churn since 2026-07-17. Rebaselined to actual + 3 (90).
rationale.mermaidOwnedBytes: Raw bytes of chunks WHOLLY owned by the Mermaid module graph (Rollup module ownership, not output file-name substrings). Mermaid stays fully precached / never-online under this explicit cap; ownership is all-or-nothing per chunk so the cap cannot absorb unrelated code. Re-measured for the PDF viewer feature (pkm-srek, 2026-07-16): actual is 3429238, still under this 3461961 limit (unchanged) though with less headroom (~0.94%) than the nominal ~4.5% convention, from unrelated drift accumulated since this cap was set rather than anything in the PDF viewer commits (no mermaid-related files changed on this branch). Limit intentionally left unchanged; worth revisiting if it goes over budget in a future unrelated change. Re-measured for KaTeX bundle hygiene (pkm-zotb, 2026-07-19): actual is 3427931, essentially unchanged -- mermaid's own module graph is untouched by the katex override (mermaid still imports katex normally, it just now resolves to the shared single install rather than its own private copy, and that copy is counted under katexOwnedBytes, not here). Limit intentionally left unchanged.
rationale.pdfjsOwnedBytes: Raw bytes of chunks WHOLLY owned by the lazy PDF viewer module graph (PdfViewer.tsx + react-pdf + pdfjs-dist; Rollup module ownership, not file-name substrings), mirroring mermaidOwnedBytes. Measured 2026-07-16 at 465880 raw (the PdfViewer-*.js chunk), confirming the ownership seeds correctly recognize the viewer chunk as wholly owned now that it exists; this replaces the deliberately-failing placeholder of 1 used while the chunk did not exist yet. Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (486845). The pdf.js worker is an emitted .mjs asset rather than a chunk, so it is bounded by largestAssetBytes/totalOutputBytes/precacheBytes instead of this cap. Re-measured for the LaTeX support feature (pkm-lr96, 2026-07-17): actual is 466849, a +969 byte drift unrelated to KaTeX (no PDF-viewer files changed on this branch), still comfortably under this 486845 limit. Limit intentionally left unchanged. Re-measured for KaTeX bundle hygiene (pkm-zotb, 2026-07-19): actual is unchanged at 466849 (the PDF viewer family is unrelated to this task). Limit intentionally left unchanged.
rationale.katexOwnedBytes: Raw bytes of chunks WHOLLY owned by the lazy KaTeX module graph (katex package modules under node_modules; Rollup module ownership, not file-name substrings), mirroring mermaidOwnedBytes/pdfjsOwnedBytes. Measured 2026-07-17 at 521352 raw -- higher than the ~250-350KB single-copy order of magnitude a single katex.min.js would suggest, because the build resolves and bundles TWO separate katex package installs: our own direct dependency katex@0.17.0 (260022 bytes, the katex-*.js chunk reached via MathSpan.tsx's `import("katex")`) plus mermaid@11.16.0's own pre-existing transitive dependency katex@0.16.47 (261329 bytes, a second katex-*.js chunk pnpm installs alongside ours since mermaid pins a different katex range -- confirmed via `pnpm why katex`). The mermaid-owned copy already shipped before this task (it was already folded anonymously into mermaidOwnedBytes/totalOutputBytes/precacheBytes); this cap is the first to call it out by name, and both chunks are verified 100% katex library code with no application code mixed in (chunkIsWhollyOwned's all-or-nothing rule holds), so the measurement is real and legitimate to cap even though it is roughly double a single copy. This replaces the deliberately-failing placeholder of 1 used while the chunk did not exist yet. Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (544813). Follow-up worth considering (not done here, out of Task 3's scope): a pnpm override/dedupe to force a single katex version would shave ~260KB off totalOutputBytes/precacheBytes and make this cap track only the new MathSpan feature. Resolved in pkm-zotb (2026-07-19): added `overrides: { katex: ^0.17.0 }` to pnpm-workspace.yaml (pnpm 11's canonical location for overrides -- package.json's pnpm.overrides is silently ignored under pnpm 11, https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/issues/11536) forcing mermaid's transitive katex@0.16.47 onto the app's own katex@0.17.0. `pnpm why katex` now reports a single resolved version, and the build emits exactly one katex-*.js chunk (260023 bytes raw, verified 100% katex library code via chunkIsWhollyOwned as before). katexOwnedBytes roughly halved as predicted: 521352 -> 260023. Confirmed mermaid diagram rendering is unaffected: mermaid's own vitest suite and the /mermaid command E2E path still pass against the shared katex@0.17.0 install (mermaid only needs KaTeX for its optional math-in-node rendering, which this app does not exercise via mermaid directly). Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (271724).
